notes:
Diamond & Pearl era set dp4 (Great Encounters, released 2008-02-13). PACK SIZE CORRECTED to 10 (NOT the EX-era 9 in the prompt baseline). Bulbapedia Booster-pack page: 'Beginning with the e-Card Series and continuing through the entirety of the EX Series, boosters had only 9 cards' but 'Since the release of the first Diamond & Pearl expansion, these consistently had 10 cards.' ToyWiz/TCGplayer/Beckett DP booster listings confirm '10 cards per pack'. The prompt's ERA note explicitly says 'confirm/adjust via sources' so I adjusted 9->10.

COMPOSITION (10 cards): 5 Common + 3 Uncommon + 1 Reverse-Holo + 1 Rare-or-better. Sourced from the Beckett DP checklist / DP booster product listings ('5 Common, 3 Uncommon, 1 Reverse Holofoil, and 1 Rare or Rare Holo'). Booster box = 36 packs (Bulbapedia Booster-pack page: 'A Booster Box: A sealed box containing 36 booster packs from a single English-language expansion'). NO ETB existed in this era -> etbPacks=null. Display product = 36-pack booster box.

CHASE = Pokemon Lv.X (rarity 'Rare Holo LV.X'). Bulbapedia Great Encounters set page confirms 4 Lv.X cards (Cresselia 103/106, Darkrai 104/106, Dialga 105/106, Palkia 106/106) and explicitly 'no Secret Rare cards' -> matches the given buckets {rare-holo:11, rare:21, uncommon:31, common:39, lv-x:4}. There is intentionally NO secret-rare bucket for this set.

RARE-OR-BETTER SLOT (the 1 rare-or-better card) resolves across {rare, rare-holo, lv-x}:
  - lv-x = 0.0278 (= 1:36 packs = exactly 1 Lv.X per 36-pack box). SOURCED: Flipside Gaming 'A Comprehensive Review of Rarity Part Two' (aggregating Pokebeach/Pokegym): 'Level X cards ... had a pull rate of approximately 1:36 packs for the first sets in the Diamond and Pearl era, and a more favorable 1:18 pull rate for later sets in the LV. X era.' Great Encounters (dp4, Feb 2008) is in the EARLY DP group (Diamond&Pearl -> Majestic Dawn), so 1:36 applies, NOT the later 1:18.
  - rare-holo = 0.324 (~1:3 packs, ~11-12 holos per box). DP-era convention for the rare slot (~1 in 3 packs holo); cross-checks the community 'roughly one holo rare per pack on average / ~36 rares per box combining holo + reverse-rare' figure surfaced in search. Scaled slightly below 0.333 to make room for the lv-x slice while keeping the holo:non-holo ratio era-typical.
  - rare = remainder = 1 - 0.0278 - 0.324 = 0.6482 (plain non-holo rare). Slot sums to exactly 1.0.

REVERSE-HOLO SLOT: per the dataset convention (matches ex-firered-leafgreen and ex-crystal-guardians configs), vintage reverse-holo filler is modeled with the plain common/uncommon ids since only their market value matters; a neutral 50/50 common/uncommon split is used. Sums to 1.0. (GE pool is common:39 / uncommon:31; neutral 0.5/0.5 kept for cross-set consistency.)

CONFIDENCE: 'low' — no per-set published pull-rate STUDY exists for this 2008 vintage set; the Lv.X 1:36 rate and the ~1:3 holo split are DP-era community/aggregator averages (Flipside aggregating Pokebeach/Pokegym, plus retailer pack-structure listings) mapped onto Great Encounters, not a GE-specific large sample. Vintage sealed GE boxes carry a heavy collector premium, so EV/booster will sit far below box price — expected; rates are NOT inflated to compensate.

INTEGRITY: every slot sums to exactly 1.0; all distribution keys {common, uncommon, rare, rare-holo, lv-x} are a strict subset of this set's available buckets {rare-holo, rare, uncommon, common, lv-x}. No invented per-set percentages — the Lv.X 1:36 and holo ~1:3 ordering are the sourced era figures.
